incident-bot: :rotating_light: PagerDuty alert fired (PD-82419) — p99 latency > 2.5s for model-serving-prod in eu-west-1. Alert rule: p99_latency_route > 2.5s for 3m. Auto-page to eng-oncall.
PagerDuty: Incident PD-82419 created and routed to Priya Nair (on-call).
Priya Nair: acked on PD, jumping in. will check recent deploys + routing.
Jon Becker: on it — looking at autoscaler + queue backlog. seeing CPU stable but GPU queue length up.
Sasha Liu: checking KV cache eviction logs and hotkeys. quick grep shows many evictions around the spike.
Priya Nair: timeline — spike started ~1785012280 per metrics, coincides with a canary rollout of model v2.3.0 about 6m ago.
Jon Becker: suspects rollout + batch config mismatch causing tail latency. reverting canary?
Priya Nair: not yet — let's try mitigation first: scale dedicated pool and reduce max_batch_size for v2.3.0. If that fails, rollback.
OpsBot: runbook link added: https://redwood/runbooks/latency-tail-spike — checklist appended to thread.
Sasha Liu: snippet from kvcache logs:
```
2026-04-05T12:10:11Z WARN kvcache eviction key=customer:hotkey:12345 size=482MB
2026-04-05T12:10:12Z INFO kvcache miss key=customer:hotkey:12345
2026-04-05T12:10:13Z ERROR serve latency_ms=2791 req_id=abcd1234
```
Jon Becker: running command to bump capacity in eu-west-1: `infractl pool resize --pool model-serving-prod-eu --add 4-gpu`
Priya Nair: doing a quick config patch to force max_batch_size=8 for v2.3.0 in canary. patch applied to 2 nodes. monitoring.
PagerDuty: PD-82419 acknowledged by Priya Nair. Escalation paused.
Sasha Liu: checking customer-facing impact — dashboards show 0.8% of requests above 2.5s in last 5m, concentrated on a small set of API keys (hotkey pattern).
Ben Carter: support here — if it's only a subset, we can route those keys to fallback model variant until we stabilize. drafting message to affected accounts.
Priya Nair: agreed, Sasha, can you publish a temporary routing rule? e.g., `route.override --key-prefix customer:hotkey --model model-v2.2.6`
Sasha Liu: done. rule applied and propagated to edge within ~45s.
incident-bot: autoscale action completed — 4 GPUs added to model-serving-prod-eu.
Jon Becker: after the scale + batch cap + hotkey reroute, p99 dropped to ~1.1s. still watching for any oscillation.
Priya Nair: can everyone pls add short reaction when they confirm their task? I want a clear handoff once stable. :white_check_mark:
Sasha Liu: confirmed — kvcache eviction rate down, hotkey reroute holding. :white_check_mark:
Jon Becker: confirmed — autoscaler scaled, queue drained. :white_check_mark:
Ben Carter: support note queued, will send once we hit 10m stable window.
Priya Nair: plan: keep increased capacity + hotkey reroute for 30m, then either lift or rollback canary based on stability. If p99 reverts >1.8s we'll rollback v2.3.0.
incident-bot: incident updated: PD-82419 state=acknowledged, priority=high, runbook=latency-tail-spike.
Priya Nair: quick postmortem items to capture in thread: (1) canary batch config mismatch; (2) lack of hotkey pre-check in rollout; (3) consider autoscaler faster scale-up policy for tail events.
Jon Becker: adding metric alert to surface hotkey evictions earlier.
Ben Carter: will prepare customer-facing summary and share draft here before send.
Priya Nair: handing off to on-call rotation next in 15m. if stable at handoff, handoff note: PD-82419 - mitigated, monitoring.
OpsBot: handoff checklist ticked by Priya Nair.
Priya Nair: nice work team. closing communication channel in 20m unless we see regression. :pray:

source.messages:
Ava Chen: Heads-up — I need to step out at 2:00pm PT today. I'm currently primary for US‑WEST until then. Secondary (Ravi) is on vacation until tomorrow. There's a maintenance window at 4:30pm PT that could cause brief error spikes. Can someone cover the 14:00–20:00 PT window or be on standby to escalate?

incident-bot: [AUTO] Maintenance scheduled 16:30 PT — expected transient latency increases for embeddings. Pager thresholds adjusted for the window.

Ravi Patel: I'm OOO today but reachable by DM/phone for P0 only. Please avoid paging me for P1/P2.

Sofia Morales: I can take 14:30–18:00 PT if that works. I'll mark myself primary in PD and leave runbook pointers here.

Ava Chen: Perfect, thank you. Quick runbook notes (copy these into the incident ticket if you open one):

```
# KV cache flush (use only during maintenance or heavy KV errors)
kubectl exec -it deployment/inference-runtime -- /bin/sh -c "service redis restart && curl -X POST https://internal-api/clear-prefix?prefix=session"
# Force fallback variant (temporary, with rollback plan)
redwoodctl rollout set --model-id rwd/gpt-small --variant fallback --force
```

DevOnCall: +1 to Sofia. Add the escalation thresholds to your head-up: if error rate > 3% sustained for 5m OR latency p99 > 1200ms -> page SRE lead and oncall-sre. Pager rules summary: P2 = thread + async notify; P1 = page primary + oncall-sre; P0 = phone page + escalate to Eng Director + all-hands bridge. If model-level stalls (KV/LLM hangs), alert eng-runtime channel immediately.

Sofia Morales: I've set myself as Primary in PD at 14:10 PT and updated my PD status message. Checklist I'm running: 1) PD primary status, 2) confirm US‑WEST routing in Console (route->US-WEST), 3) pre-warm KV cache, 4) leave handoff note in ticket.

incident-bot: [AUTO] PagerDuty status change detected — Sofia Morales set as Primary at 14:10 PT.

Ava Chen: Customer-impact note: two customers (acme-corp, fastchat) currently have long-running streams in US‑WEST. If we flip to fallback variants, notify Support and the customers. Use this template: https://redwood.internal/docs/incident-templates (see "streaming-fallback-notify")

Sofia Morales: Where's the support template? grabbing link now — https://redwood.internal/docs/incident-templates#streaming-fallback-notify. I'll post to #support if we flip.

Ava Chen: Escalation matrix (short): P2 = async in thread + tag oncall channel; P1 = page primary + @oncall-sre; P0 = phone -> all-hands bridge + page execs. For infra-level drops (redis/kv) page eng-infra oncall immediately.

DevOnCall: Operation note: please tag incidents opened during this window with `handoff-note:true` and include these runbook steps. Also capture a brief `customer_impact: true/false` line in the ticket root.

Sofia Morales: Pre-warm complete. Running KV flush in ~10m (during low traffic). Will post results. :thumbsup:

Ava Chen: Thanks everyone. Final handoff checklist for the covering period: 1) confirm PD primary, 2) monitor error spikes at 16:30 window, 3) run KV flush if errors persist, 4) notify Support + customers if fallback used, 5) escalate to Ravi (phone) for P0.

Sofia Morales: Copy — will monitor and escalate per matrix.

DevOnCall: Logged: handoff handover added to oncall notes. If anything looks like systemic regression, open a P1 and @channel the SRE lead.

Ava Chen: Leaving now — thanks Sofia. DM me if you need my runbook snippet or quick context.

source.messages:
incident-bot: PagerDuty alert triggered: PD-INV-18872 - inference-proxy edge 5xx burst (threshold: 5m error rate > 4%). Link: <https://pagerduty.com/incidents/PD-INV-18872>
Ava Chen: acked — I'm oncall, investigating. pinging infra to check autoscaler and edge pool.
Sam Torres: seeing a 3–4 minute spike on router-503s for us-west-2, error-rate jump from 0.3% -> 3.6%. latency p95 unchanged. pulling traces now.
Priya Rao: FYI PD escalated to @ops-escalation at +2min. any customer-facing 5xx counts?
Sam Torres: quick query against prod-logs: most 5xx are from internal probes / synthetic checks hitting a new health endpoint. example log line: `GET /_healthcheck 500 from 10.12.8.9`
Luca Bianchi: hm looks like the synthetic runner hit a bad binary and started spamming probes. I've temporarily added a predicate to drop probe traffic at the proxy: `edge.filter.drop_tag == "synth-check"` — rolling at 3 hosts first.
Ava Chen: good call. please comment when rule is live. I'll keep PD acked and watch.
Luca Bianchi: rule is live on canary (host-12,13,14) — error-rate on those hosts dropped to baseline. rolling to remaining hosts in 60s.
pagerduty-bot: Incident PD-INV-18872 acknowledged by Ava Chen.
Sam Torres: dashboards show overall cluster 5xx returning to normal as roll continues. p95 latency steady. looks like noisy-synthetic causing flapping alerts not real customer impact.
Priya Rao: can we silence PD for the alert group until the synthetic owner rolls a fix? otherwise we keep getting pages.
Ava Chen: yes please. applying soft-silence for the alert rule 10m while we verify.
incident-bot: Soft-silence applied to alert group `edge-5xx-spike` for 10m by Ava Chen.
pagerduty-bot: PagerDuty note: alert auto-resolve window started (no further triggers).
Sam Torres: confirm no 5xx spikes in real user traffic (prod-edge logs filtered by UA not containing "synth"): nothing substantial in last 10m.
Luca Bianchi: created a follow-up ticket: INF-4891 to roll back bad synthetic and add better retires on the runner.
Priya Rao: action item: reduce synthetic cadence to avoid bulk probe bursts. assigned to synthetic infra.
Ava Chen: closing thread — alert auto-resolved, PD incident closed (auto-resolve). No customer impact determined. If it flaps again we'll escalate to oncall rotation and do full postmortem. :thumbsup:
pagerduty-bot: Incident PD-INV-18872 automatically resolved (status: resolved)
incident-bot: Note: this thread logged as a transient flapping page caused by synthetic probe noise. Recommend follow-up with synthetic team and consider adding a filter to the alert to ignore known-probe IPs.
Priya Rao: I'll open a short blameless note and attach graphs. tagging @synth-team.
Sam Torres: TL;DR: transient synthetic probe loop produced bursty 5xx; quick in-line edge filter + soft-silence mitigated; no customer impact; INF-4891 created.
Ava Chen: thanks all — monitoring for 30m then will hand back to normal rotation.

Priya Rao: FYI PD escalated to @ops-escalation at +2min. any customer-facing 5xx counts?
Sam Torres: quick query against prod-logs: most 5xx are from internal probes / synthetic checks hitting a new health endpoint. example log line: `GET /_healthcheck 500 from 10.12.8.9`
Luca Bianchi: hm looks like the synthetic runner hit a bad binary and started spamming probes. I've temporarily added a predicate to drop probe traffic at the proxy: `edge.filter.drop_tag == "synth-check"` — rolling at 3 hosts first.
Ava Chen: good call. please comment when rule is live. I'll keep PD acked and watch.
Luca Bianchi: rule is live on canary (host-12,13,14) — error-rate on those hosts dropped to baseline. rolling to remaining hosts in 60s.
pagerduty-bot: Incident PD-INV-18872 acknowledged by Ava Chen.
Sam Torres: dashboards show overall cluster 5xx returning to normal as roll continues. p95 latency steady. looks like noisy-synthetic causing flapping alerts not real customer impact.
Priya Rao: can we silence PD for the alert group until the synthetic owner rolls a fix? otherwise we keep getting pages.
Ava Chen: yes please. applying soft-silence for the alert rule 10m while we verify.
incident-bot: Soft-silence applied to alert group `edge-5xx-spike` for 10m by Ava Chen.
pagerduty-bot: PagerDuty note: alert auto-resolve window started (no further triggers).
Sam Torres: confirm no 5xx spikes in real user traffic (prod-edge logs filtered by UA not containing "synth"): nothing substantial in last 10m.
Luca Bianchi: created a follow-up ticket: INF-4891 to roll back bad synthetic and add better retires on the runner.
Priya Rao: action item: reduce synthetic cadence to avoid bulk probe bursts. assigned to synthetic infra.
Ava Chen: closing thread — alert auto-resolved, PD incident closed (auto-resolve). No customer impact determined. If it flaps again we'll escalate to oncall rotation and do full postmortem. :thumbsup:
pagerduty-bot: Incident PD-INV-18872 automatically resolved (status: resolved)
incident-bot: Note: this thread logged as a transient flapping page caused by synthetic probe noise. Recommend follow-up with synthetic team and consider adding a filter to the alert to ignore known-probe IPs.
Priya Rao: I'll open a short blameless note and attach graphs. tagging @synth-team.
Sam Torres: TL;DR: transient synthetic probe loop produced bursty 5xx; quick in-line edge filter + soft-silence mitigated; no customer impact; INF-4891 created.
Ava Chen: thanks all — monitoring for 30m then will hand back to normal rotation.

source.messages:
Rafael Mendes: want to sync on a recurring point of confusion: when does Support move an escalation from #support into #incidents vs keeping it in #support + paging oncall? seeing a few cases where we ended up with 2 parallel threads.

Dev Patel: +1. Support’s default is “start in #support, page if P0/P1”, but sometimes oncall immediately asks us to move it. would love a rule of thumb we can paste into the playbook.

Owen Phillips: from Support side: we’re trying not to spam #incidents for single-customer stuff, but we also don’t want to hide a real incident. examples we’ve struggled with: streaming resets (Acme, us-east) vs “unexpected 429 after SDK upgrade” (Aurora).

Sean Gallagher: agree we need a clean trigger. #incidents should be for anything that looks like platform degradation OR will require coordinated mitigation/comms. #support is fine for “help me debug this customer” cases.

Mina Haddad: +1. especially for routing/fallback questions—Support often needs quick confirmation of “is fallback active?” that can stay in #support if it’s just interpretation. but if fallback is activating unexpectedly at scale, that’s incident territory.

Rafael Mendes: concrete proposal:
1) start in #support for single-account triage / suspected client-side behavior
2) move to #incidents when any of the below is true:
- suspected regional/service degradation (elevated 5xx, latency, timeouts) even if only 1 customer reported so far
- multiple customers or multiple tickets reporting same symptom in short window
- needs an oncall-led mitigation (traffic shift, rollback, capacity move, config change)
- needs status page / broad comms decision
- exceeds paging threshold (aligned to SLO alerts) OR we’re going to page more than one team

Priya Shah: can we add “Private installer upgrades stuck” as an example? typically 1 customer, but requires a coordinated eng response + careful rollback steps.

Sean Gallagher: for Private: if it’s one customer blocked on upgrade, that’s usually #support + page the Private Deployments oncall (not necessarily #incidents) unless it’s a release-wide regression or we expect more customers to hit it.

Owen Phillips: makes sense. so “customer-specific but high touch” != automatically #incidents.

Dev Patel: what about embedded timeouts eu-west (like SUP-1850-style)? started as single customer, but ended up being partial degradation.

Rafael Mendes: that’s a good “start in #support but move fast” case. if the first triage checks show signals outside the customer (dashboards, error rate, synthetic), we should move to #incidents early. we lose time when we wait for “2nd customer confirmation.”

Mina Haddad: +1. also, for routing/fallback: if Support sees pinned model “ignored” (SUP-1847-type), that can stay in #support until we confirm it’s widespread. if we see unexpected fallback activations or policy violations across customers/tiers, #incidents.

Dylan Brooks: tooling angle: if we do move to #incidents, can we standardize the handoff? like: link SUP ticket(s), copy the incident template, pin request IDs + region + model. otherwise #incidents becomes scrollback archaeology.

Rafael Mendes: yes. we’re already adding “required context” for pages; same should apply for moving to #incidents.

incident-bot: Reminder: For active incidents, use /incident start in #incidents to create an incident record and status updates. (If you started in another channel, link it here.)

Owen Phillips: ok but can we be explicit about what *doesn’t* go to #incidents? we’ve had oncall ask us to move things that are basically docs / SDK troubleshooting.

Sean Gallagher: examples that should stay in #support:
- 429/quota confusion after SDK upgrade (until proven backend regression)
- structured output validation errors / schema issues
- SSO login loop for one tenant (unless auth provider outage)
- webhook SSL validation failures where it’s customer cert chain

Dev Patel: and examples that should go to #incidents:
- streaming disconnects spiking in a region (even if only 1 enterprise customer notices first)
- rerank endpoint 500s on large batches if we see elevated 5xx
- “console dashboards missing regions” only if telemetry pipeline is broken for multiple customers

Mina Haddad: one nuance: #incidents isn’t the same as “page SRE”. we can have an incident w/ Platform owning. but #incidents is still the right place when we need one shared source of truth + comms.

Rafael Mendes: exactly. #incidents is coordination + decision log. paging is separate.

Priya Shah: what about update cadence? Support sometimes keeps it in #support because they’re doing customer updates and don’t want to overcomplicate.

Sean Gallagher: if it’s in #incidents, it actually helps. we can align: who is customer-facing writer, what’s approved to say, and when to post status page vs docs banner.

Dev Patel: ok I’m sold. can we write the “move to #incidents” criteria in one pasteable block?

Rafael Mendes: draft pasteable guidance (feel free to edit):
“Keep in #support when: it’s a single-customer issue with no evidence of platform degradation; suspected client config/SDK usage; needs targeted debugging.
Move to #incidents when: suspected service/regional degradation; multiple customers affected; mitigation requires coordinated eng action (rollback/traffic shift/capacity); we may post a status update or need cross-team coordination. When moving: link all SUP tickets, paste incident context (customer/tier, region, model, request IDs, start time, impact), and pin it.”

Owen Phillips: :thumbsup: add one line: “Don’t wait for 2nd customer if dashboards show it.”

Rafael Mendes: +1. adding.

Dylan Brooks: I’ll align Slack workflow with this. INT-332/INT-333 already tracking the automation; we can add a step that prompts “Move to #incidents? (Y/N)” w/ the criteria + link to template.

Dev Patel: I’ll update the escalation playbook section + share in #support. also will add the examples list (429 vs streaming vs private upgrade).

Sean Gallagher: please include the note that #incidents is not a backdoor to bypass incident process. once it’s there, we should use /incident if it’s truly incident-worthy; otherwise keep it in #support.

Mina Haddad: and for routing-specific escalations, add “if fallback is active for a single customer, confirm in #support; if fallback activation is unexpected or widespread, move to #incidents.”

Rafael Mendes: cool. I’ll post a summary and we’ll treat this as the default.

Rafael Mendes: SUMMARY / agreement:
- Default start: #support for triage.
- Move to #incidents when there’s evidence/suspicion of platform or regional degradation, multi-customer impact, need for coordinated mitigation/comms, or cross-team paging.
- Don’t wait for a second report if dashboards/synthetics indicate an issue.
- Private “single customer blocked” stays in #support unless it’s a release-wide regression.
- When moving to #incidents: link SUP tickets + paste/pin context (region/model/request IDs/impact) and use /incident when appropriate.

Dev Patel: :thumbsup: will incorporate verbatim.

source.text:
Rafael Mendes: hey folks — quick ask from on-call side. we’re getting paged from Support with partial context and it slows triage a lot. can we standardize what goes into a page / escalation message? ideally a pinned template Support can copy/paste into bridges + PD notes.

Owen Phillips: +1. we’ve been trying to push “request IDs + region” but it’s not consistently included. what’s your must-have list?

Rafael Mendes: must-have for any page (Hosted/Dedicated/Private):
- customer/tier (self-serve/dedicated/private/enterprise)
- impact summary (what broke, % failures, latency, etc.)
- *request IDs* (>=3) + approx time window (with timezone)
- region + endpoint (chat/embeddings/rerank/etc.)
- model + version (and whether pinned)
- error snippets (sanitized) + HTTP status codes
- what’s already been tried (retries, model swap, region swap, etc.)
- any “recent changes” signal (deploy in last 2h / runtime version change / config rollout)
- links: SUP ticket + bridge thread

Dev Patel: makes sense. for “recent changes” can Support reliably know deploys? we can add “not sure / please confirm” but don’t want to block paging.

Rafael Mendes: totally fine if unknown. even “we don’t know if there was a deploy” is useful vs silence. if you can include “customer noticed starting ~30 mins ago” that helps correlate.

Priya Shah: can you drop the exact template you want? we can pin in #support and also bake into the Slack bridge workflow (INT-332).

Rafael Mendes: yep — proposed copy/paste:
```
[PAGING CONTEXT] (P0/P1) <short summary>
Customer: <name> | Tier: <hosted/dedicated/private/enterprise>
Scope: <# users/tenants affected> | Regions: <us-east/eu-west/...>
Endpoint: <chat/completions/embeddings/rerank> | SDK: <py/ts/go + version>
Model: <name> | Version/pinned?: <yes/no + version>
Impact: <error rate %, latency p95 delta, “streams reset mid-gen”, etc.>
Time window: <start-end> <timezone>
Request IDs (3+):
- req_...
- req_...
- req_...
Errors (sanitized):
- <status code + message>
What we tried / current mitigation:
- <retry/backoff, region failover, disable http2, etc.>
Recent changes?: <unknown / runtime X.Y / config rollout link>
Links:
- Jira SUP-####: <link>
- Slack thread/bridge: <link>
- Dashboards (if any): <link>
```
key thing: request IDs + model/version + region. those three are the difference between “we can act” and “we have to ask 5 questions”.

Sean Gallagher: +1 from SRE. also helps us decide quickly whether this is a “move to #incidents” situation vs keep in support bridge.

Owen Phillips: looks good. question: do you want “severity” too or just Support priority?

Rafael Mendes: include Support priority (P0–P4). if you also know suspected Sev (Sev0–Sev3) that’s fine, but don’t bikeshed. we can map on our side.

Priya Shah: for request IDs — sometimes customers only give timestamps + correlation id in their app logs. ok to page without IDs?

Rafael Mendes: if it’s truly impossible, page with:
- customer’s IP ranges (if relevant)
- their org_id / project_id (internal) if Support can pull it
- exact timestamps + region + endpoint
but please label “NO REQUEST IDs AVAILABLE” so we know to route to log search differently.

Mina Haddad: can we add one line for “fallback/routing involved?” we’ve seen confusion on pinned versions vs fallback.

Rafael Mendes: good call. add:
“Routing/fallback: <unknown/observed> | customer sees pinned version?: <yes/no>”
(and if you have it, link the routing/fallback dashboard).

Dev Patel: action items:
1) we’ll pin this template in #support + #customer-success
2) add to escalation playbook page + bridge workflow prompt (INT-332)
3) update Jira intake checklist to require: region, model, request IDs, time window

Owen Phillips: :thumbsup: i’ll also nudge the team to include at least 3 request IDs every time. we’ll add a macro snippet.

Sean Gallagher: thanks. if you want a hard rule: any P0/P1 page missing request IDs gets bounced back w/ “need IDs to proceed” unless it’s clearly a broad outage.

Rafael Mendes: +1. broad outage exception: if multiple customers + elevated error rates, page anyway and we’ll correlate via metrics.

Priya Shah: cool. i’ll draft the pinned message + send for quick review here before posting.

Rafael Mendes: sounds good. :pray: this will save us a ton of cycles on the next streaming incident.

- req_...
- req_...
- req_...
Errors (sanitized):
- <status code + message>
What we tried / current mitigation:
- <retry/backoff, region failover, disable http2, etc.>
Recent changes?: <unknown / runtime X.Y / config rollout link>
Links:
- Jira SUP-####: <link>
- Slack thread/bridge: <link>
- Dashboards (if any): <link>
```
key thing: request IDs + model/version + region. those three are the difference between “we can act” and “we have to ask 5 questions”.

Sean Gallagher: +1 from SRE. also helps us decide quickly whether this is a “move to #incidents” situation vs keep in support bridge.

Owen Phillips: looks good. question: do you want “severity” too or just Support priority?

Rafael Mendes: include Support priority (P0–P4). if you also know suspected Sev (Sev0–Sev3) that’s fine, but don’t bikeshed. we can map on our side.

Priya Shah: for request IDs — sometimes customers only give timestamps + correlation id in their app logs. ok to page without IDs?

Rafael Mendes: if it’s truly impossible, page with:
- customer’s IP ranges (if relevant)
- their org_id / project_id (internal) if Support can pull it
- exact timestamps + region + endpoint
but please label “NO REQUEST IDs AVAILABLE” so we know to route to log search differently.

Mina Haddad: can we add one line for “fallback/routing involved?” we’ve seen confusion on pinned versions vs fallback.

Rafael Mendes: good call. add:
“Routing/fallback: <unknown/observed> | customer sees pinned version?: <yes/no>”
(and if you have it, link the routing/fallback dashboard).

Dev Patel: action items:
1) we’ll pin this template in #support + #customer-success
2) add to escalation playbook page + bridge workflow prompt (INT-332)
3) update Jira intake checklist to require: region, model, request IDs, time window

Owen Phillips: :thumbsup: i’ll also nudge the team to include at least 3 request IDs every time. we’ll add a macro snippet.

Sean Gallagher: thanks. if you want a hard rule: any P0/P1 page missing request IDs gets bounced back w/ “need IDs to proceed” unless it’s clearly a broad outage.

Rafael Mendes: +1. broad outage exception: if multiple customers + elevated error rates, page anyway and we’ll correlate via metrics.

Priya Shah: cool. i’ll draft the pinned message + send for quick review here before posting.

Rafael Mendes: sounds good. :pray: this will save us a ton of cycles on the next streaming incident.
text:
Rafael Mendes: hey folks — quick ask from on-call side. we’re getting paged from Support with partial context and it slows triage a lot. can we standardize what goes into a page / escalation message? ideally a pinned template Support can copy/paste into bridges + PD notes.

Owen Phillips: +1. we’ve been trying to push “request IDs + region” but it’s not consistently included. what’s your must-have list?

Rafael Mendes: must-have for any page (Hosted/Dedicated/Private):
- customer/tier (self-serve/dedicated/private/enterprise)
- impact summary (what broke, % failures, latency, etc.)
- *request IDs* (>=3) + approx time window (with timezone)
- region + endpoint (chat/embeddings/rerank/etc.)
- model + version (and whether pinned)
- error snippets (sanitized) + HTTP status codes
- what’s already been tried (retries, model swap, region swap, etc.)
- any “recent changes” signal (deploy in last 2h / runtime version change / config rollout)
- links: SUP ticket + bridge thread

Dev Patel: makes sense. for “recent changes” can Support reliably know deploys? we can add “not sure / please confirm” but don’t want to block paging.

Rafael Mendes: totally fine if unknown. even “we don’t know if there was a deploy” is useful vs silence. if you can include “customer noticed starting ~30 mins ago” that helps correlate.

Priya Shah: can you drop the exact template you want? we can pin in #support and also bake into the Slack bridge workflow (INT-332).

Rafael Mendes: yep — proposed copy/paste:
```
[PAGING CONTEXT] (P0/P1) <short summary>
Customer: <name> | Tier: <hosted/dedicated/private/enterprise>
Scope: <# users/tenants affected> | Regions: <us-east/eu-west/...>
Endpoint: <chat/completions/embeddings/rerank> | SDK: <py/ts/go + version>
Model: <name> | Version/pinned?: <yes/no + version>
Impact: <error rate %, latency p95 delta, “streams reset mid-gen”, etc.>
Time window: <start-end> <timezone>
Request IDs (3+):
- req_...
- req_...
- req_...
Errors (sanitized):
- <status code + message>
What we tried / current mitigation:
- <retry/backoff, region failover, disable http2, etc.>
Recent changes?: <unknown / runtime X.Y / config rollout link>
Links:
- Jira SUP-####: <link>
- Slack thread/bridge: <link>
- Dashboards (if any): <link>
```
key thing: request IDs + model/version + region. those three are the difference between “we can act” and “we have to ask 5 questions”.

- exact timestamps + region + endpoint
but please label “NO REQUEST IDs AVAILABLE” so we know to route to log search differently.

“Routing/fallback: <unknown/observed> | customer sees pinned version?: <yes/no>”
(and if you have it, link the routing/fallback dashboard).

1) we’ll pin this template in #support + #customer-success
2) add to escalation playbook page + bridge workflow prompt (INT-332)
3) update Jira intake checklist to require: region, model, request IDs, time window

Owen Phillips: :thumbsup: i’ll also nudge the team to include at least 3 request IDs every time. we’ll add a macro snippet.

Sean Gallagher: thanks. if you want a hard rule: any P0/P1 page missing request IDs gets bounced back w/ “need IDs to proceed” unless it’s clearly a broad outage.

source.content:
Rafael Mendes: heads up, running the perf-canary *disable switch drill* now (us-east only). goal: confirm we can shut it off cleanly without touching inference endpoints + without alert spam. anyone around to sanity check dashboards/metrics?

Sean Gallagher: here. what’s the expected “done” signal? QPS goes to 0 + no 5xx on inference + alerts auto-resolve?

Rafael Mendes: yep. checklist:
1) set `perf_canary.enabled=false` in control-plane for us-east targets
2) confirm canary pods scale to 0 (or go idle) + request rate drops
3) ensure inference /internal endpoints show no elevated error rate
4) ensure we don’t generate new warning/paging alerts from missing data
5) re-enable after ~10 mins

Noah Patel: I’m on too. disable path is via control-plane config (not helm). should be a no-op for runtime except fewer internal requests.

Omar El-Sayed: I can do the flip. which config key are we using in prod again? (I have the dashboard open but don’t want to fat-finger)

Rafael Mendes: `canary_targets` in control-plane, and there’s a top-level `enabled` boolean for the region. use the “safe-disable” toggle we added (keeps config validated but stops scheduling runs).

Vanessa Ortiz: watching fleet metrics (gpu util) in us-east. canary overhead should drop ~0.2-0.3% if all working.

Logan Wright: I’ll watch telemetry ingestion and metric cardinality. also want to confirm that “no data” doesn’t trip the regression evaluator.

Aisha Bello: I’m on Grafana: Per-Model Canary Overview (us-east). I’ll verify request count -> 0 and that alert panels don’t go red.

Allison Grant: reminder: alerts should be written as “if present” + min samples. missing timeseries should not page. if we see any alert behavior on missing data, we need to fix that ASAP.

Omar El-Sayed: ok doing it now.

ConfigBot: Rafael Mendes requested change in control-plane config.
ConfigBot: Diff summary:
- region=us-east
- perf_canary.enabled: true -> false
- audit: requested_by=omar.e, approved_by=rafael.m

Omar El-Sayed: change submitted, waiting for propagation. expected within ~2 mins.

Noah Patel: while we wait, I’m tailing runtime request logs for canary SA. should just stop. if we see auth failures or retries, call it out.

Sean Gallagher: can we also confirm incident-bot dedupe doesn’t create a “resolved” spam thread?

Rafael Mendes: yep. if we get any bot noise, capture screenshots + we’ll file follow-up.

Aisha Bello: seeing canary request rate trending down already. panels still green.

Vanessa Ortiz: gpu util in us-east dedicated pool dropped a tiny amount (~0.15%) in last 5 min. consistent w canary backing off.

Logan Wright: telemetry looks normal. no ingestion errors. I do see the `perf_canary_run_total` counter flatlining (expected).

Grafana: Alert state change: perf-canary-warning-us-east (model=top_model_1) -> OK

Aisha Bello: we got one alert resolving (not firing). looks fine.

Sean Gallagher: do we expect any “no data” warning? sometimes Grafana does that.

Aisha Bello: not seeing it. alert rule uses “absent() == 0” guard + min samples, so should be ok.

Noah Patel: runtime logs: canary traffic stopped. no increase in 5xx/429 on internal endpoints.

Omar El-Sayed: canary k8s: deployment still exists but replicas went to 0 via HPA min=0 when enabled=false (it stops scheduling so HPA scales down). confirming with:
```
kubectl -n perf-canary get deploy
kubectl -n perf-canary get hpa
```

Rafael Mendes: nice. let’s hold disabled for ~10 mins and confirm stability.

Connor O'Brien: just joining. this “disable switch” is the one releases would use if we see bad behavior mid-rollout, right? (vs rolling back chart)

Rafael Mendes: yes. intended as fastest stop: no helm changes, no restart required. control-plane toggle only.

Connor O'Brien: perfect. if this stays clean, I’ll update the release checklist to reference this drill outcome.

Allison Grant: can someone check that paging rules don’t interpret missing canary as “regression”? (seen that pattern elsewhere)

Logan Wright: looking at evaluator metrics: `perf_canary_regression_eval_total` is not incrementing. no “regression=true” labels emitted. so it’s not evaluating when disabled.

Sean Gallagher: can we do a quick inference endpoint health check? ideally from outside the runtime team.

Rafael Mendes: good call. I’ll hit a lightweight internal generate call (tiny prompt) against the same model endpoints canary uses.

Rafael Mendes: ran 20 requests across 2 models (us-east) via internal client. all 200s, latencies normal. no error spikes.

Aisha Bello: dashboards: `canary_qps` ~0, `tokens/sec` panels show “no data” but panel status is fine, no alert banners.

Vanessa Ortiz: gpu util steady. no weird sawtooth. looks like the disable is truly stopping the load.

Omar El-Sayed: leaving disabled for 3 more mins then re-enable.

Noah Patel: +1. note: when re-enabled we should see a warmup ramp (rate limiter) not instant fanout.

Omar El-Sayed: re-enabling now (same toggle).

ConfigBot: Change applied in control-plane config.
ConfigBot: Diff summary:
- region=us-east
- perf_canary.enabled: false -> true
- audit: requested_by=omar.e, approved_by=rafael.m

Aisha Bello: seeing request rate tick up slowly (good). no alert fires.

Logan Wright: evaluator ticks resumed. metrics are coming back without new label explosions.

Grafana: Alert state change: perf-canary-warning-us-east (model=top_model_1) -> Pending

Aisha Bello: Grafana shows Pending (expected while min samples accumulates). shouldn’t notify.

Sean Gallagher: looks like drill passed. can we capture the “expected behavior” in the runbook? esp: replicas to 0, no data doesn’t alert, re-enable ramps.

Rafael Mendes: yep. action items:
- [Rafael] update runbook with disable steps + validation signals
- [Connor] update release checklist to reference control-plane toggle
- [Allison/Aisha] add a note in alerting policy doc: missing canary data should not page

Noah Patel: thanks all. from runtime side: no impact observed on inference endpoints. disable switch looks safe.

Omar El-Sayed: :thumbsup: I’ll also add a tiny “canary_enabled” gauge metric so dashboards show explicitly disabled vs missing ingestion (would’ve made this less ambiguous).

Sean Gallagher: +1 on the gauge. closing the drill from oncall perspective. good work.

source.text:
Sean Gallagher: Syncing on oncall readiness for auto-rollback-on-SLO-breach. Need to lock: (1) what alerts page vs notify, (2) incident-bot message format/targets, (3) who owns first response when an auto action fires.

Rafael Mendes: +1. Also want to avoid paging storms during rollout-heavy periods. We should codify: successful rollback is *noise* unless it’s frequent or rollback fails.

Aisha Bello: I can outline the alert rules we’re planning in observability-pack PR. Draft set:
- `RolloutAutoActionTriggered` (labels: service, route, rollout_id, action=pause|rollback)
- `RolloutAutoActionFailed` (executor error / idempotency failure)
- `RolloutSLOMonitorStaleOrMissing` (adapter couldn’t fetch burn-rate status or sample size)
- `RolloutSLOEvalFlapping` (optional: evaluation toggling breach/ok too often)

Allison Grant: Reminder: label cardinality guardrails. `rollout_id` should not be on *paging* alerts unless we’re confident we won’t blow up PD incident grouping. For Slack notifications it’s fine.

Logan Wright: Telemetry-wise, we can include `rollout_id` in the event stream and let incident-bot handle formatting + linking. For alertmanager, we can group by `service, route, region, env`.

Connor O'Brien: From release side: feature is behind flag until we sign off on paging + runbook. Would prefer PD only for failures / missing monitors during active rollouts.

Sean Gallagher: Proposed routing:
1) Successful auto-pause/rollback => Slack notify only (no page)
2) Rollback executor failure => PAGE inference-platform oncall
3) Monitor missing/stale => notify initially; page only if rollout is active + we’re in “auto actions enabled” mode (not dry-run)

Rafael Mendes: What about “suppressed” cases? Like known incident window or admin override. Those should notify (so we don’t forget we’re running without guardrail).

Aisha Bello: Yup. We have `decision=suppressed` w/ `suppress_reason` label in the metric, but we should be careful w/ label explosion. Probably encode reason as enum: `known_incident`, `admin_override`, `holdoff`, `min_samples`, `telemetry_degraded`.

Allison Grant: Please keep `suppress_reason` bounded set only. No freeform strings.

Chloe Martin: On ownership: if an auto-rollback triggers for a customer-facing route, who’s first responder? Platform SRE or service owner? This touches Console + control plane.

Sean Gallagher: For MVP: Inference Platform SRE is first response for the *system* (rollout controller / policy engine / monitor adapter / executor). Service owner is pulled in only if it’s their rollout and we need root cause.

Jada Williams: That works. Most of the time the action is “pause” and someone needs to decide to resume anyway. But failures (can’t shift traffic back) are system-level.

Rafael Mendes: We should spell it out:
- If auto action occurred and completed => service owner gets notified in their channel (no page). SRE stays unpaged.
- If auto action failed or got stuck => page platform SRE. Platform SRE engages oncall from controller/policy/console as needed.

Connor O'Brien: +1. Also release checklist should require the service team to provide `#channel` to notify (or default `#eng-platform` + `#eng-oncall`).

Aisha Bello: Alerting detail proposal (Grafana/Alertmanager):
- `RolloutAutoActionFailed` severity=page
  - condition: `sum(rate(rollout_auto_action_total{outcome="failed"}[5m])) > 0`
  - group_by: service, env, region
- `RolloutSLOMonitorStaleOrMissing` severity=notify (then page if >15m during active rollout)
  - condition notify: `sum(rate(rollout_slo_monitor_status_total{status=~"stale|missing"}[10m])) > 0`
  - condition page: `... and max_over_time(rollout_active{auto_actions="enabled"}[15m]) == 1`
- `RolloutAutoActionTriggered` severity=notify only
  - condition: any increase over 5m

Allison Grant: Looks reasonable. One more: `RolloutAutoActionRateHigh` notify-only to catch thrash. Like >3 auto actions per service per 30m.

Logan Wright: For “active rollout” signal: do we have a reliable metric? If not, we can approximate via controller state metric `rollout_step_current`.

Jada Williams: Controller emits `rollout_state{state="running"}` gauge per rollout today, but it includes rollout_id (cardinality). We can add an aggregated `rollout_active{service,route,region,env}` gauge without rollout_id.

Chloe Martin: For incident-bot formatting, can we include:
- action (pause/rollback)
- policy name + version
- SLOs breached (latency p99, error rate)
- link to rollout page in Console
- link to dashboard scoped to service/route/region

incident-bot: FYI: PR-477 adds posting for `rollout.auto_action.*` events. Default target channels currently: `#incidents-auto-mitigation` and `#eng-oncall` (configurable).

Sean Gallagher: Great. We might want `#eng-oncall` only for failures + missing monitors. For normal “rollback succeeded” we can route to service channel + `#eng-sre` maybe.

Rafael Mendes: Agree. #eng-oncall is already noisy.

Aisha Bello: We can set it as:
- `action=rollback|pause` AND `outcome=succeeded` => post to service owner channel + `#eng-sre` (no PD)
- `outcome=failed` => post to `#eng-oncall` + PD page
- `decision=suppressed` => post to `#eng-sre` + service channel (no PD) w/ suppress_reason

Connor O'Brien: How does incident-bot find “service owner channel”? Do we have a mapping?

Chloe Martin: Console has service registry metadata (owner team + slack channel) in the config service. If missing, fallback to `#eng-platform`.

Logan Wright: We should make the fallback explicit and audit it. Also we should include a warning in the message when owner channel is missing.

Allison Grant: Example message text (keep it short, link-heavy):

```
[Auto-rollback] rollout_id=rl_9b2c… service=inference-gateway route=/v1/chat region=us-east-1 env=prod
Action: rollback -> model=llama-3.3-70b-v5 (from v6)
Reason: SLO breach (p99 latency burn-rate fast=14.2x, slow=3.1x) + error_rate fast=9.8x
Policy: default-prod-dedicated v3 | Decision trace: dt_1a7…
Console: https://console.redwood.ai/rollouts/rl_9b2c…
Dash: https://grafana/... (scoped)
Runbook: https://confluence/.../auto-rollback-on-slo-breach
```

Rafael Mendes: +1. Also include “next step”: e.g., “Rollout paused at step 3/5” or “Traffic returned to baseline 100%”.

Jada Williams: Executor can emit `traffic_split_before` / `traffic_split_after` in audit event. incident-bot can pull that.

Sean Gallagher: Paging policy:
- `RolloutAutoActionFailed` => Page “Inference Platform Oncall” (PD service: `platform-inference`) and auto-open incident if impacts prod.
- `Rollback triggered on >N services in 10m` => Page (could be systemic telemetry issue or bad policy rollout)
- Everything else => Slack notify

pagerduty-bot: Note: PD service `platform-inference` escalation policy currently pages Primary -> Secondary -> Manager. If we add new alert, please include `routing_key` and `dedup_key` standards.

Aisha Bello: Dedup key suggestion: `auto_rollback_failed:{env}:{service}:{region}`. That should group repeated failures for same service.

Rafael Mendes: And for monitor missing/stale page condition: make sure it doesn’t trigger during known telemetry incident. We should tie into the “do not rollback during known incident” override.

Sean Gallagher: Yep. For paging we can gate on `telemetry_health=ok` or suppression state. If telemetry is degraded, we should notify but not page (unless it’s widespread).

Logan Wright: Telemetry health signal exists: `telemetry_pipeline_status{component="slo-toolkit"}`. We can add a simple boolean in incident-bot too.

Connor O'Brien: Action items:
- Aisha: finalize alert severities + routing proposal in ENG-4840 and link here
- Jada/Logan: confirm aggregated `rollout_active` metric without rollout_id
- Chloe: confirm service registry -> slack channel mapping is available to incident-bot
- Sean/Rafael: update runbook section “Who is on point” + “When does this page?”

Sean Gallagher: I’ll also add a launch gate: no prod enablement until (1) dashboards + alerts merged, (2) runbook published, (3) incident-bot routing configured, (4) PD dedupe verified in staging.

Rafael Mendes: :thumbsup: Let’s do a quick tabletop after staging E2E to validate paging behavior.

grafana-bot: Alert rule preview created: `RolloutAutoActionFailed` (severity=page) in folder `Auto Rollback`. Pending review by @Aisha Bello.

Aisha Bello: Will review today. Also FYI: I’ll set `RolloutAutoActionTriggered` to notify-only and route to service channel + `#eng-sre` by default.

Chloe Martin: One more edge case: if Console UI is down, people will click Console links and hit 500. Can we include raw audit event id + a direct Kibana link?

Logan Wright: Yes. We can include `audit_event_id` + link to logs in Honeycomb. I’ll ping incident-bot PR author.

Sean Gallagher: Cool. Closing this out once we have the final routing config in incident-bot and the runbook updated.

Action: rollback -> model=llama-3.3-70b-v5 (from v6)
Reason: SLO breach (p99 latency burn-rate fast=14.2x, slow=3.1x) + error_rate fast=9.8x
Policy: default-prod-dedicated v3 | Decision trace: dt_1a7…
Console: https://console.redwood.ai/rollouts/rl_9b2c…
Dash: https://grafana/... (scoped)
Runbook: https://confluence/.../auto-rollback-on-slo-breach
```

Rafael Mendes: +1. Also include “next step”: e.g., “Rollout paused at step 3/5” or “Traffic returned to baseline 100%”.

Jada Williams: Executor can emit `traffic_split_before` / `traffic_split_after` in audit event. incident-bot can pull that.

Sean Gallagher: Paging policy:
- `RolloutAutoActionFailed` => Page “Inference Platform Oncall” (PD service: `platform-inference`) and auto-open incident if impacts prod.
- `Rollback triggered on >N services in 10m` => Page (could be systemic telemetry issue or bad policy rollout)
- Everything else => Slack notify

pagerduty-bot: Note: PD service `platform-inference` escalation policy currently pages Primary -> Secondary -> Manager. If we add new alert, please include `routing_key` and `dedup_key` standards.

Aisha Bello: Dedup key suggestion: `auto_rollback_failed:{env}:{service}:{region}`. That should group repeated failures for same service.

Rafael Mendes: And for monitor missing/stale page condition: make sure it doesn’t trigger during known telemetry incident. We should tie into the “do not rollback during known incident” override.

Sean Gallagher: Yep. For paging we can gate on `telemetry_health=ok` or suppression state. If telemetry is degraded, we should notify but not page (unless it’s widespread).

Logan Wright: Telemetry health signal exists: `telemetry_pipeline_status{component="slo-toolkit"}`. We can add a simple boolean in incident-bot too.

Connor O'Brien: Action items:
- Aisha: finalize alert severities + routing proposal in ENG-4840 and link here
- Jada/Logan: confirm aggregated `rollout_active` metric without rollout_id
- Chloe: confirm service registry -> slack channel mapping is available to incident-bot
- Sean/Rafael: update runbook section “Who is on point” + “When does this page?”

Sean Gallagher: I’ll also add a launch gate: no prod enablement until (1) dashboards + alerts merged, (2) runbook published, (3) incident-bot routing configured, (4) PD dedupe verified in staging.

Rafael Mendes: :thumbsup: Let’s do a quick tabletop after staging E2E to validate paging behavior.

grafana-bot: Alert rule preview created: `RolloutAutoActionFailed` (severity=page) in folder `Auto Rollback`. Pending review by @Aisha Bello.

Aisha Bello: Will review today. Also FYI: I’ll set `RolloutAutoActionTriggered` to notify-only and route to service channel + `#eng-sre` by default.

Chloe Martin: One more edge case: if Console UI is down, people will click Console links and hit 500. Can we include raw audit event id + a direct Kibana link?

Logan Wright: Yes. We can include `audit_event_id` + link to logs in Honeycomb. I’ll ping incident-bot PR author.

Sean Gallagher: Cool. Closing this out once we have the final routing config in incident-bot and the runbook updated.
text:
Sean Gallagher: Syncing on oncall readiness for auto-rollback-on-SLO-breach. Need to lock: (1) what alerts page vs notify, (2) incident-bot message format/targets, (3) who owns first response when an auto action fires.

Rafael Mendes: +1. Also want to avoid paging storms during rollout-heavy periods. We should codify: successful rollback is *noise* unless it’s frequent or rollback fails.

Aisha Bello: I can outline the alert rules we’re planning in observability-pack PR. Draft set:
- `RolloutAutoActionTriggered` (labels: service, route, rollout_id, action=pause|rollback)
- `RolloutAutoActionFailed` (executor error / idempotency failure)
- `RolloutSLOMonitorStaleOrMissing` (adapter couldn’t fetch burn-rate status or sample size)
- `RolloutSLOEvalFlapping` (optional: evaluation toggling breach/ok too often)

Allison Grant: Reminder: label cardinality guardrails. `rollout_id` should not be on *paging* alerts unless we’re confident we won’t blow up PD incident grouping. For Slack notifications it’s fine.

Logan Wright: Telemetry-wise, we can include `rollout_id` in the event stream and let incident-bot handle formatting + linking. For alertmanager, we can group by `service, route, region, env`.

Connor O'Brien: From release side: feature is behind flag until we sign off on paging + runbook. Would prefer PD only for failures / missing monitors during active rollouts.

Sean Gallagher: Proposed routing:
1) Successful auto-pause/rollback => Slack notify only (no page)
2) Rollback executor failure => PAGE inference-platform oncall
3) Monitor missing/stale => notify initially; page only if rollout is active + we’re in “auto actions enabled” mode (not dry-run)

Rafael Mendes: What about “suppressed” cases? Like known incident window or admin override. Those should notify (so we don’t forget we’re running without guardrail).

Aisha Bello: Yup. We have `decision=suppressed` w/ `suppress_reason` label in the metric, but we should be careful w/ label explosion. Probably encode reason as enum: `known_incident`, `admin_override`, `holdoff`, `min_samples`, `telemetry_degraded`.

Allison Grant: Please keep `suppress_reason` bounded set only. No freeform strings.

Chloe Martin: On ownership: if an auto-rollback triggers for a customer-facing route, who’s first responder? Platform SRE or service owner? This touches Console + control plane.

Sean Gallagher: For MVP: Inference Platform SRE is first response for the *system* (rollout controller / policy engine / monitor adapter / executor). Service owner is pulled in only if it’s their rollout and we need root cause.

Jada Williams: That works. Most of the time the action is “pause” and someone needs to decide to resume anyway. But failures (can’t shift traffic back) are system-level.

Rafael Mendes: We should spell it out:
- If auto action occurred and completed => service owner gets notified in their channel (no page). SRE stays unpaged.
- If auto action failed or got stuck => page platform SRE. Platform SRE engages oncall from controller/policy/console as needed.

Connor O'Brien: +1. Also release checklist should require the service team to provide `#channel` to notify (or default `#eng-platform` + `#eng-oncall`).

Aisha Bello: Alerting detail proposal (Grafana/Alertmanager):
- `RolloutAutoActionFailed` severity=page
  - condition: `sum(rate(rollout_auto_action_total{outcome="failed"}[5m])) > 0`
  - group_by: service, env, region
- `RolloutSLOMonitorStaleOrMissing` severity=notify (then page if >15m during active rollout)
  - condition notify: `sum(rate(rollout_slo_monitor_status_total{status=~"stale|missing"}[10m])) > 0`
  - condition page: `... and max_over_time(rollout_active{auto_actions="enabled"}[15m]) == 1`
- `RolloutAutoActionTriggered` severity=notify only
  - condition: any increase over 5m

Allison Grant: Looks reasonable. One more: `RolloutAutoActionRateHigh` notify-only to catch thrash. Like >3 auto actions per service per 30m.

Logan Wright: For “active rollout” signal: do we have a reliable metric? If not, we can approximate via controller state metric `rollout_step_current`.

Jada Williams: Controller emits `rollout_state{state="running"}` gauge per rollout today, but it includes rollout_id (cardinality). We can add an aggregated `rollout_active{service,route,region,env}` gauge without rollout_id.

Chloe Martin: For incident-bot formatting, can we include:
- action (pause/rollback)
- policy name + version
- SLOs breached (latency p99, error rate)
- link to rollout page in Console
- link to dashboard scoped to service/route/region

incident-bot: FYI: PR-477 adds posting for `rollout.auto_action.*` events. Default target channels currently: `#incidents-auto-mitigation` and `#eng-oncall` (configurable).

Sean Gallagher: Great. We might want `#eng-oncall` only for failures + missing monitors. For normal “rollback succeeded” we can route to service channel + `#eng-sre` maybe.

Rafael Mendes: Agree. #eng-oncall is already noisy.

Aisha Bello: We can set it as:
- `action=rollback|pause` AND `outcome=succeeded` => post to service owner channel + `#eng-sre` (no PD)
- `outcome=failed` => post to `#eng-oncall` + PD page
- `decision=suppressed` => post to `#eng-sre` + service channel (no PD) w/ suppress_reason

Connor O'Brien: How does incident-bot find “service owner channel”? Do we have a mapping?

Chloe Martin: Console has service registry metadata (owner team + slack channel) in the config service. If missing, fallback to `#eng-platform`.

Logan Wright: We should make the fallback explicit and audit it. Also we should include a warning in the message when owner channel is missing.

Allison Grant: Example message text (keep it short, link-heavy):

```
[Auto-rollback] rollout_id=rl_9b2c… service=inference-gateway route=/v1/chat region=us-east-1 env=prod
Action: rollback -> model=llama-3.3-70b-v5 (from v6)
Reason: SLO breach (p99 latency burn-rate fast=14.2x, slow=3.1x) + error_rate fast=9.8x
Policy: default-prod-dedicated v3 | Decision trace: dt_1a7…
Console: https://console.redwood.ai/rollouts/rl_9b2c…
Dash: https://grafana/... (scoped)
Runbook: https://confluence/.../auto-rollback-on-slo-breach
```

Rafael Mendes: +1. Also include “next step”: e.g., “Rollout paused at step 3/5” or “Traffic returned to baseline 100%”.

Jada Williams: Executor can emit `traffic_split_before` / `traffic_split_after` in audit event. incident-bot can pull that.

Sean Gallagher: Paging policy:
- `RolloutAutoActionFailed` => Page “Inference Platform Oncall” (PD service: `platform-inference`) and auto-open incident if impacts prod.
- `Rollback triggered on >N services in 10m` => Page (could be systemic telemetry issue or bad policy rollout)
- Everything else => Slack notify

pagerduty-bot: Note: PD service `platform-inference` escalation policy currently pages Primary -> Secondary -> Manager. If we add new alert, please include `routing_key` and `dedup_key` standards.

Aisha Bello: Dedup key suggestion: `auto_rollback_failed:{env}:{service}:{region}`. That should group repeated failures for same service.

Rafael Mendes: And for monitor missing/stale page condition: make sure it doesn’t trigger during known telemetry incident. We should tie into the “do not rollback during known incident” override.

Sean Gallagher: Yep. For paging we can gate on `telemetry_health=ok` or suppression state. If telemetry is degraded, we should notify but not page (unless it’s widespread).

Logan Wright: Telemetry health signal exists: `telemetry_pipeline_status{component="slo-toolkit"}`. We can add a simple boolean in incident-bot too.

Connor O'Brien: Action items:
- Aisha: finalize alert severities + routing proposal in ENG-4840 and link here
- Jada/Logan: confirm aggregated `rollout_active` metric without rollout_id
- Chloe: confirm service registry -> slack channel mapping is available to incident-bot
- Sean/Rafael: update runbook section “Who is on point” + “When does this page?”

Sean Gallagher: I’ll also add a launch gate: no prod enablement until (1) dashboards + alerts merged, (2) runbook published, (3) incident-bot routing configured, (4) PD dedupe verified in staging.

Rafael Mendes: :thumbsup: Let’s do a quick tabletop after staging E2E to validate paging behavior.

grafana-bot: Alert rule preview created: `RolloutAutoActionFailed` (severity=page) in folder `Auto Rollback`. Pending review by @Aisha Bello.

Aisha Bello: Will review today. Also FYI: I’ll set `RolloutAutoActionTriggered` to notify-only and route to service channel + `#eng-sre` by default.

Chloe Martin: One more edge case: if Console UI is down, people will click Console links and hit 500. Can we include raw audit event id + a direct Kibana link?

Logan Wright: Yes. We can include `audit_event_id` + link to logs in Honeycomb. I’ll ping incident-bot PR author.

Sean Gallagher: Cool. Closing this out once we have the final routing config in incident-bot and the runbook updated.

source.content:
Sean Gallagher: seeing elevated tail latency in Hosted us-east-1 starting ~10 min ago. p99 gen latency +35-45% on `llama-3.1-70b-instruct` route. errors not spiking, but queue time is up. anyone around who can help apply the kernel selection v2 rollback runbook?

Incident Bot: (auto) Alert fired: runtime_latency_p99_regression (service=hosted, region=us-east-1) current=1.92s baseline=1.28s. Correlated dimension: model=llama-3.1-70b-instruct.

Yuki Sato: yep. does it correlate w/ selector v2 being enabled for that model in us-east-1? check the selection distribution panel (v1 vs v2) + chosen kernel.

Sean Gallagher: pulling dashboard now. selector_v2_enabled appears true for llama-3.1-70b-instruct in us-east-1 (canary flag). selection distribution shifted in last 15m: a bunch of traffic moved to `flash_attn_v3_prefill` on L40S pool.

Omar El-Sayed: that smells like the long-context mis-bucket we saw earlier. do we have confirmation it’s only us-east-1 + that model?

Sean Gallagher: quick slice: us-west-2 looks stable. other models in us-east-1 stable-ish. seems isolated to llama-3.1-70b-instruct.

Yuki Sato: ok. runbook says first action: disable v2 at the narrowest scope (model+region) via gates allow/denylist, then validate p95/p99 + selection distro returns to v1.

Ethan Park: i can do the gate flip on control plane. confirm exact identifiers: region `us-east-1`, model slug `llama-3.1-70b-instruct` (not the base).

Sean Gallagher: confirmed. slug in metrics: `llama-3.1-70b-instruct`.

Ethan Park: applying deny rule now.

Ethan Park: using policy-gate CLI:
`rwctl gate set kernel_selector_v2 --region us-east-1 --model llama-3.1-70b-instruct --state disabled --reason "p99 regression; rollback per runbook"`

Ethan Park: change propagated to config service; should hot reload on runtimes within ~60s.

Allison Grant: :eyes: pls also note sampling rate so we don’t lose the selection telemetry we need for postmortem. we can keep telemetry on while v2 disabled (should still emit v1 fields).

Omar El-Sayed: if you can, grab a couple selection dump events before/after for sanity (candidate set + chosen kernel).

Sean Gallagher: ack. i’m going to wait 2-3 mins then check:
1) selector_v2_enabled false in us-east-1 for that model
2) selection distro shifts back to v1 kernels
3) p99 starts trending down

Yuki Sato: also check queue time vs compute time. if mis-selection caused slower prefill, compute time should drop after flip.

Sean Gallagher: 2 min later:
- selector_v2_enabled now false (model+region)
- selection distro: `flash_attn_v3_prefill` dropped from ~62% to ~4%, back to `flash_attn_v2_prefill`
- p99 is improving: 1.92s -> 1.54s (still above baseline but trending)
- compute_ms down ~18%

Omar El-Sayed: good. give it another 5-10m for full traffic mix + caches to settle.

Ethan Park: gate change is scoped only to us-east-1 + that model. no other models touched.

Sean Gallagher: :thumbsup: thanks. i’m adding a note in oncall log.

Sean Gallagher: 8 min after flip:
- p95 back to baseline range
- p99 now 1.30-1.35s (baseline 1.28s)
- error rate unchanged
looks recovered.

Yuki Sato: nice. please keep the deny rule in place until we patch thresholds / add L40S-specific guardrail. can you paste the dashboard link + a screenshot of the before/after selection distro in the incident thread? (or just the query).

Sean Gallagher: will do. query i used:
`selection_enabled{region="us-east-1",model="llama-3.1-70b-instruct"}` and
`kernel_choice_share{region="us-east-1",model="llama-3.1-70b-instruct"} by (kernel)`

Allison Grant: can we also add an annotation to the latency dashboard w/ the gate flip timestamp? helps later when we review.

Ethan Park: i’ll add an annotation in the rollout dashboard + link the gate change ID.

Omar El-Sayed: follow-up: we should pull a few selection telemetry samples from right before the flip (L40S + long-context requests) and see which feature bucket it thought it was in.

Yuki Sato: agreed. i’ll open a quick bug to adjust the seq bucket boundary or add a deny rule for that kernel on L40S until verified.

Sean Gallagher: cool. for now i’m marking alert as mitigated; keeping close watch for next 30m. thanks all.

Incident Bot: (auto) Alert resolved: runtime_latency_p99_regression (service=hosted, region=us-east-1). duration=00:22:11. note: mitigated by disabling kernel_selector_v2 for model=llama-3.1-70b-instruct in us-east-1.

source.body:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: ALERT: PagerDuty P0 fired for service=redwood-inference-prod, symptom=queue_backlog_high. queue_length=12k, tail_latency_95=2.4s, drop_rate=4.8%. Trigger: autoscaler_pending_nodes > 10m. PD link: https://pagerduty.example/incidents/PD123456

PagerDuty: Incident PD123456 created and routed to eng-oncall. auto-escalation in 3m.

Rohan Patel: acked via PagerDuty. I'm on it — looking at autoscaler metrics now.

Ava Chen: saw spike ~20:03 UTC, likely after a rollout for kvcache change. Queue jump correlated with model-hotstart failures?

Samir Gupta: logs show many `gpu-alloc` requests stuck in Pending due to node provisioning throttles from cloud quota. Also leader autoscaler logs: `scale_decision skipped: insufficient node quota` repeatedly.

Maya (SRE): customer impact: Hosted API 95p latency doubled; some streaming responses failing with timeout. Customer-facing error rate ~5% (mostly small customers on shared pool).

Rohan Patel: quick mitigation plan: 1) trigger manual surge of worker replicas 2) fall back to smaller model variant for non-critical routes 3) evict large batch jobs. Trying manual scale now.

Rohan Patel: ```bash
# manual scale
kubectl -n inference-prod scale deployment/worker-pool --replicas=60
kubectl -n inference-prod annotate deployment/worker-pool redwood/manual-scale="true"
```

OpsRelay: automated surge job kicked off: created 8 ephemeral GPU nodes (p3.xlarge equivalent). ETA ~90s.

Ava Chen: I'm toggling routing policy to prefer dedicated pool for enterprise customers to reduce impact on hosted tier. Will post config snippet.

Ava Chen: ```json
{"routing":{"policy":"prefer_dedicated","overrides":[{"route":"/v1/generate","target":"dedicated-pool-enterprise"}]}}
```

Samir Gupta: dropping non-essential heavy batches: `killjob --selector=slot=batch-heavy --grace=10` — killing 7 jobs.

Maya (SRE): putting note in status page draft: "Investigating increased latency and request failures for text generation. Some customers may see degraded performance."

Rohan Patel: manual scale completed, replicas=60, pods becoming Ready. Watching queue.

incident-bot: autoscaler controller reports: `scale_decision: resumed` at 20:06:12 UTC.

Rohan Patel: queue length decreased from 12k -> 3.2k in 2m after scale and batch kills. Tail latency 95p down to 1.1s. Drop rate -> 1.3%.

PagerDuty: PD123456 acknowledged by Rohan Patel. Escalation canceled.

Ava Chen: routing change applied, enterprise routes now on dedicated. Verified 200s for a sample of responses.

Samir Gupta: follow-up: autoscaler root cause appears to be twofold — cloud provider rate-limit throttled node provisioning (quota bursts) and our kvcache warmup increased burst of cold-start memory pressure. Filed infra ticket INFRA-789.

Maya (SRE): impact summary for postmortem: - Duration: 00:07m from page to stable baseline - Affected: hosted API customers, small fraction (~5%) errors - Mitigation: manual scale + batch kills + routing overrides - Action items: add autoscaler soft-surge reserve, pre-warm kvcache during canary, add cloud quota monitors.

Rohan Patel: handing off long-running follow-ups to Ava (next on-call). I'll stay for 10m to confirm no regression. Ava, can you take the postmortem and runbook updates?

Ava Chen: taking handoff. I'll open postmortem and schedule 1:1 with infra to discuss quota. Thanks all. :thumbsup:

incident-bot: Incident PD123456 marked as resolved by Rohan Patel at 20:10:02 UTC.

Maya (SRE): leaving an ops note: `kvcache: enable gradual warmup flag; autoscaler: implement immediate soft-surge` (refs INFRA-789, REX-402).

Samir Gupta: adding monitoring alert: `cloud.node.provision.throttle` to integrate with triage runbook.

Ava Chen: posting postmortem link when ready. Good job triaging quickly.

Maya (SRE): leaving an ops note: `kvcache: enable gradual warmup flag; autoscaler: implement immediate soft-surge` (refs INFRA-789, REX-402).

Samir Gupta: adding monitoring alert: `cloud.node.provision.throttle` to integrate with triage runbook.

Ava Chen: posting postmortem link when ready. Good job triaging quickly.

source.content:
Rafael Mendes: escalating to kernels team per runbook. perf-canary paging alert looks like a real regression (not queueing). can we get eyes from @Hiro Tanaka ASAP?

incident-bot: Paging alert (Sev2): perf-canary sustained regression
- model: llama-3.1-70b-instruct
- region: us-east
- tier: hosted
- metric: runtime_p95_ms
- baseline: 1480ms
- observed: 1885ms (+27.4%)
- confidence: high (n=620 over 18m)
- tokens/sec: baseline 118 tok/s -> observed 96 tok/s (-18.6%)
Links: grafana: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/perf-canary?var-model=llama-3.1-70b-instruct&var-region=us-east | traces: https://traces.redwood.ai/search?svc=perf-canary&model=llama-3.1-70b-instruct&region=us-east | runbook: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/SRE/Runbook+-+Perf+Regression+Canary+Alert

Aisha Bello: looking at the dashboard now. queueing_time_p95 is basically flat (tiny bump but not enough). decode_p95 jumped.

Aisha Bello: quick numbers from the panel:
- e2e_p95 +24-28%
- runtime_only_p95 +25%
- queueing_p95 +2-3%
- batch_size dist unchanged
KV cache hit rate also unchanged. smells like kernel path change.

Sean Gallagher: +1. if it’s a kernel auto-select flip we should feature-flag it off for this model while we work on proper revert.

Noah Patel: I can confirm this lines up with runtime build rwd-2026.02.01-17 (rolled to us-east ~40 min ago). model version unchanged.

Noah Patel: also seeing kernel tag in metrics: `attn_kernel=fa_v3` shows up for most canary requests post-rollout; baseline was mostly `fa_v2`.

Rafael Mendes: @Hiro Tanaka can you check if we changed FA selection defaults in the last build? regression is isolated to llama-3.1-70b-instruct (so far), A100 pool.

Hiro Tanaka: here. yes, we enabled fa_v3 auto-select for a few architectures behind a “safe” gate, but it’s still supposed to be allowlisted per-model.

Hiro Tanaka: can you paste the runtime tags from one trace? want to confirm it’s actually picking the new kernel variant (there are two).

Aisha Bello: from trace attributes:
```
runtime_build=rwd-2026.02.01-17
hw_sku=a100-80g
model=llama-3.1-70b-instruct@2026-01-15
attn_kernel=flashattn_v3
attn_kernel_variant=v3_r2
compile_profile=prod_default
```

Sofia Ivanova: scheduler side looks stable. no signs of deeper batching changes. queue is flat; prefill/decode runtime is where the delta is.

Omar El-Sayed: corroborating from metrics store: `runtime_decode_ms` histogram shifted right after build. no cardinality weirdness.

Hiro Tanaka: ok that’s the v3_r2 path. we did see a perf cliff on longer decode on that variant in internal microbench but thought it was fixed. apparently not.

Hiro Tanaka: immediate mitigation options:
1) flip feature flag to force fa_v2 for this model+sku (fastest)
2) rollback the runtime build in us-east (heavier)

Hiro Tanaka: I’d recommend (1) first. Do we have control-plane override wired for `kernel_profile` per model?

Noah Patel: yes. we can pin `kernel_profile=fa_v2` via control-plane config. It’s in the same surface as canary target config.

Rafael Mendes: cool. can we do that now to stop the bleed while we validate? @Connor O'Brien / @Vanessa Ortiz can you help coordinate change + safe rollout?

Vanessa Ortiz: here. if it’s config-only (no binary deploy) we can push it w/ audit log in ~5-10m. we should scope it to us-east + a100 first.

Connor O'Brien: +1. please avoid full runtime rollback unless config mitigation doesn’t work.

Aisha Bello: do we need to also disable it for other models proactively? (top-5) or just this one?

Hiro Tanaka: start with this model. keep an eye on other top models; if we see it there too we can widen.

Hiro Tanaka: what key are you using? it should be:
- model: `llama-3.1-70b-instruct`
- hw: `a100-80g`
- override: `attention.kernel=flashattn_v2`
(or `kernel_profile=fa_v2` depending on which layer your config uses)

Noah Patel: control plane uses `kernel_profile`. I’ll propose:
`kernel_profile: fa_v2`
`selector: { region: us-east, hw_sku: a100-80g }`
`reason: perf-canary regression 1769961611`

Rafael Mendes: sounds good. please paste the change request / link when up.

Vanessa Ortiz: made change in control-plane (pending approval): https://control.redwood.ai/audit/changes/CP-88421
Diff summary: pin llama-3.1-70b-instruct + us-east + a100-80g -> kernel_profile=fa_v2

Sean Gallagher: approving from SRE side.

Connor O'Brien: approved. rollout starts now.

Omar El-Sayed: watching metrics for kernel tag flip. should see `attn_kernel=fa_v2` within a few minutes once pods pick config.

Aisha Bello: perf-canary stream already showing fewer `fa_v3` tags. decode p95 trending down.

Noah Patel: canary last 5m window:
- runtime_p95_ms 1885 -> 1608 (still elevated but improving)
- tokens/sec 96 -> 109
Kernel tag mix now ~80% fa_v2.

Hiro Tanaka: good. remaining elevation could be mixed cache / a few stragglers still on old config. give it 10-15m.

Rafael Mendes: ok. next step is kernel-side fix / revert, right?

Hiro Tanaka: yup. I’m going to:
- disable v3_r2 for llama-3.1-70b class in the allowlist
- open a revert PR for the auto-select change in rwd-2026.02.01-17 if needed

Hiro Tanaka: also need someone to attach this incident to the kernel ticket so we track the follow-up.

Sean Gallagher: I’ll add notes to the incident channel + update status page internally. also we should keep paging suppressed for this model until stable? (warn-only)

Aisha Bello: agree. once p95 is back within +10% for 30m, we can downgrade.

Omar El-Sayed: now seeing steady-state:
- kernel tag 99% fa_v2
- runtime_p95_ms 1515 (baseline 1480, within noise)
- tokens/sec 116 (baseline 118)
Looks resolved.

Rafael Mendes: awesome. thanks all. @Hiro Tanaka please drop the PR/ticket link when you have it so oncall can reference. we’ll leave the pin in place until kernel fix lands.

Hiro Tanaka: will do. creating PR now; will post in thread + in #eng-runtime once up.

source.messages:
Rafael Mendes: Dropping the runbook draft here for review before we flip paging on phase 1. Mainly want eyes on the decision tree + escalation expectations. Doc: https://confluence.redwood.dev/eng-sre/runbooks/runbook-perf-regression-canary-alert

Rafael Mendes: TL;DR structure is:
1) Validate alert (real vs noise)
2) Classify: queueing vs runtime vs infra
3) Mitigate: rollback / disable canary / freeze
4) Escalate w/ a clean payload

Sean Gallagher: Thanks. Skimmed. It’s close, but I want it to be more “do X then Y” and less narrative. Oncall at 3am won’t read paragraphs.

Aisha Bello: +1. The decision tree graphic is helpful but we should add explicit thresholds in each branch (even if they’re “see alert payload”). E.g. “prefill_p95 up but decode flat” etc.

Noah Patel: Looking now. One thing: runbook should say what to check in the canary payload first: model, region, tier, hardware_sku, runtime_build, kernel_id, scheduler_version.

Allison Grant: Also make sure links are consistent. In the alert we’ll have Grafana panel + trace explorer + baseline artifact link. Runbook should say “start from alert links, don’t go hunting.”

Rafael Mendes: Got it. I can rewrite top section as a checklist + move the narrative to “Background” at bottom.

Sofia Ivanova: In the classifier section, we should be explicit about queueing-time interpretation. We’ve seen folks confuse “end-to-end p95” with “runtime-only p95”.

Sofia Ivanova: Suggest a table like:
- If queue_ms_p95 ↑ and runtime_ms_p95 flat → capacity/scheduling/backpressure
- If runtime_ms_p95 ↑ and queue_ms flat → kernel/runtime regression
- If both ↑ → could be fleet saturation or routing shift

Omar El-Sayed: + add “batch_size distribution changed?” That’s a smoking gun for scheduler heuristics change or sudden traffic mix shift.

Hiro Tanaka: Kernel escalation: please include when to page kernels vs file ticket. I don’t want kernel oncall spam for warn-only alerts.

Sean Gallagher: Agree. My proposal:
- Warn alert: post in #eng-runtime w/ context, no page
- Page alert: page SRE primary + runtime oncall; kernel oncall only if runtime_ms regression and kernel_id changed in same window

Aisha Bello: Can we put this in an explicit escalation matrix block? Like rows=severity, cols=teams.

Rafael Mendes: Yes. I’ll add “Escalation Matrix” section. Can someone confirm the runtime oncall alias? Is it @runtime-oncall or @serving-runtime-oncall?

Noah Patel: It’s @serving-runtime-oncall (routes to Noah/Sofia/Hiro rotation).

Allison Grant: Make sure we don’t assume access to internal dashboards. Oncall doc should mention “if links 403, ask #eng-platform-oncall or open INT ticket.” We’ve had that bite folks.

Vanessa Ortiz: Infra/fleet angle: runbook should include “check recent GPU fleet changes in region” + “check routing policy change / weight shift”. We can link to Fleet Change Log.

Vanessa Ortiz: We have a canonical dashboard for that: https://grafana.redwood.dev/d/fleet-changes (needs VPN). Add it as optional step.

Connor O'Brien: For mitigation: the runbook should state what actions are safe without release eng approval.
- Safe: pause rollout gate / hold further regions
- Needs approval: rollback runtime build, change kernel flag, change routing weights

Sean Gallagher: + include the “disable switch” for canary itself. If canary is causing load or alert storm, oncall should know how to set canary.enabled=false per region.

Rafael Mendes: Do we want oncall to disable canary in prod directly? Or only SRE can?

Sean Gallagher: SRE oncall can, but the runbook should say “only if overhead > budget OR alert storm confirmed.” Otherwise we lose signal.

Aisha Bello: Can you add a quick check for overhead? Something like “perf-canary GPU budget panel should be <0.5% per region” with the exact Grafana panel link.

Noah Patel: Another thing: the “Validate alert” step should include minimum sample size. E.g., if observed_n < min_samples, it’s likely noise. People tend to overreact.

Allison Grant: Yes, and include “anti-flap is already applied” so oncall doesn’t manually wait 30m unless the rule indicates it.

Hiro Tanaka: Small but important: when escalating to kernels, include kernel_id + arch + seq_len bucket if we have it. Otherwise we can’t reproduce.

Omar El-Sayed: We have seq_len_bucket and prompt_shape_id tags in canary metrics. Runbook should explicitly say to paste those into the thread.

Sofia Ivanova: On the decision tree, I’d also add a branch for telemetry pipeline delay. We had one case where baseline fetch lagged and the alert fired “late” with confusing timestamps.

Rafael Mendes: Good call. I’ll add: “If dashboards show flat but alert claims regression, check telemetry ingestion delay panel / statuspage.”

Connor O'Brien: Rollback guidance: runbook should mention when to revert runtime build vs when to revert config (scheduler knobs). We can’t always roll back runtime quickly.

Noah Patel: Maybe:
- If regression aligns with runtime_build change → rollback runtime (if within canary rollout window)
- If aligns with config rollout (batching heuristic) → flip feature flag / config revert
- If aligns with fleet change → engage infra, consider draining bad nodes

Sean Gallagher: + include comms expectation: for a page-level sustained regression on top models, open incident in #incidents and update every 30m. Warn-level stays in #eng-runtime thread.

Aisha Bello: Please add explicit “Definition of sustained” (e.g. 2 consecutive windows / 20 min). That’s currently implicit.

Rafael Mendes: Ack. I’ll incorporate these edits today and repost for final sign-off.

Allison Grant: When you repost, can you also add a “Copy/paste template” for the incident thread? Like:
Model:
Region:
Tier:
Hardware:
Observed:
Baseline:
Runtime build:
Kernel id:
Queue vs runtime breakdown:
Mitigation taken:

Rafael Mendes: Yep, will do.

Hiro Tanaka: Once updated, tag me on the kernel escalation section. Want to make sure it’s crisp.

Sean Gallagher: Same for SRE actions. I want to ensure we’re not asking oncall to do anything risky without guardrails.

Rafael Mendes: Sounds good. Targeting EOD. :thumbsup: (will post updated link in this thread)

Aisha Bello: :thumbsup: 

Noah Patel: :thumbsup:
