source.thread:
Connor O'Brien: ok aligning on rollout checklist for new cache telemetry (prefix + KV + evictions + wasted prefill). want this in one place for oncall. goal: staged -> canary -> prod with clear gates + rollback triggers.

Connor O'Brien: proposed steps:
1) staging enable + verify metrics exist
2) perf-canary gate (latency/CPU)
3) prod canary 1% hosted (1 region)
4) expand to 10% hosted (2 regions)
5) prod 100% hosted
6) then dedicated/private opt-in

Connor O'Brien: need owners + exact gates. @Allison Grant @Sean Gallagher @Marcus Reed @Logan Wright @Caleb Johnson @Omar El-Sayed

Allison Grant: thx. also want explicit “what dashboards to watch” + “what do we do if dashboards show zeros” (we hit that before w label drops)

Sean Gallagher: +1. and call out what’s pager vs ticket. hit-rate dipping alone shouldn’t page unless we’re confident telemetry is real + it’s impacting $ or p95.

Marcus Reed: perf side: we have perf-canary job wired to fail if p95 regresses >0.7% on the standard chat workload w telemetry enabled. runtime overhead goal is basically noise. I’d be more conservative for prod rollout: rollback if p95>1% sustained 15m in canary.

Omar El-Sayed: runtime flags: 
- `cache_telemetry_v2_enabled` (master switch)
- `cache_telemetry_sample_rate` (default 0.1 in prod canary, can push to 0.25 after we’re confident)
- `cache_telemetry_trace_annotations_enabled` (keep on in canary only initially)

Omar El-Sayed: also important: counters are per request lifecycle; we fixed the stream retry double count + batched prefill attribution. but I still want a canary gate that compares “hits + misses ~= lookups” within tolerance.

Logan Wright: ingestion side: cardinality guards are live behind allowlist. we will drop forbidden labels and emit a “dropped_labels_total” metric. rollout gate should include: dropped series % < 0.5% and total series count within budget.

Caleb Johnson: yup. also rate limits: if event volume spikes, agent will start sampling down + emit `telemetry_rate_limited_total`. that’s a rollback trigger if it’s non-zero for sustained period (means we’re in a bad place cost-wise or something’s mis-tagged).

Sergio Costa: dashboards are in the new “Cache Telemetry v2” folder. panels include:
- KV hit-rate (overall + by miss_reason)
- Prefix reuse depth histogram
- Evictions + memory pressure gauge
- Wasted prefill rate
- Telemetry health: ingest lag + dropped labels + rate-limited

Sergio Costa: we can pin a “canary view” with filters: env=prod, rollout=canary, region=us-east-1, model_family + route_bucket.

Aisha Bello: for alerting, I’d rather not alert on hit-rate itself during rollout. but we should alert on telemetry health regressions (missing data, ingest lag, sudden zeros) + latency overhead.

Connor O'Brien: ok let’s write the actual checklist.

Connor O'Brien: STAGING
- flip `cache_telemetry_v2_enabled=true` in staging
- set sample=1.0 in staging
- validate we see: kv_lookup_total, kv_hit_total, kv_miss_total, eviction_total, wasted_prefill_tokens_total (names per standards)
- validate miss_reason breakdown present w allowed set
- validate no raw prompt logging (should just be buckets)

Omar El-Sayed: add correctness checks (staging): run the integration test workload w repeated prompts. expect hit-rate > 80% after warm, and “version_mismatch” ~0. if version_mismatch > 1% something’s wrong w join keys.

Logan Wright: staging ingest check: series count under expected (no per-tenant explosion). we can query “top label combinations by series count”. if route_id isn’t bucketing we’ll see it immediately.

Allison Grant: can we add “if dashboards show zeros”: first check telemetry health panel -> if ingest_lag>0 or dropped_labels skyrockets, treat as pipeline issue not cache regression.

Connor O'Brien: PERF-CANARY (pre-prod gate)
- merge/build runtime w flags default off
- run perf-canary workload (PR-287)
- PASS criteria:
  - p95 latency delta <= +0.7%
  - CPU overhead <= +1% on gateway + runtime nodes (from canary summary)
  - no spike in allocs / GC pauses (runtime note)

Marcus Reed: and fail hard on p99 too: p99 delta <= +1.0% (we’ve seen “small avg overhead” hide tail issues)

Connor O'Brien: PROD CANARY 1% (Hosted)
- enable flag for 1% traffic in us-east-1 only
- sample=0.1
- keep trace annotations on for canary only
- watch for 60 minutes

Connor O'Brien: CANARY GATES (must all be green)
A) perf:
- p95 latency delta <= +1% (15m rolling)
- error rate delta <= +0.1% absolute
B) telemetry health:
- ingestion lag < 2m
- `dropped_labels_total` / `emitted_samples_total` < 0.5%
- `telemetry_rate_limited_total` == 0 (or < 1/min, but ideally 0)
C) correctness sanity:
- kv_hit + kv_miss ~= kv_lookup within 2% (per route bucket)
- miss_reason distribution sane (no single reason suddenly 100% unless we expect)

Sean Gallagher: + “pager vs ticket” decision:
- PAGE oncall if: latency gate fails OR errors increase OR telemetry missing across all canary (health panel red) because we lose visibility mid-rollout
- FILE ticket if: hit-rate looks different but telemetry health is green (likely workload mix) unless it’s extreme + correlated w cost spike.

Sergio Costa: I can add an alert that triggers during canary if “canary telemetry present == false” for >10m (aka all zeros). that’s a page.

Allison Grant: rollback triggers should be explicit and reversible steps:
ROLLBACK (immediate):
- disable `cache_telemetry_v2_enabled` for canary segment
- keep old metrics untouched
- if ingestion overloaded, also set sample=0 (belt & suspenders)
- notify #eng-platform and #eng-runtime w link to canary dashboard snapshot

Caleb Johnson: also if we hit series budget: ingestion will start dropping. rollback trigger: series_count > budget by 20% OR dropped_labels > 2% for 10m. (dropping is data quality issue)

Omar El-Sayed: runtime rollback note: disabling flag should fully stop emit. no restart required, but config propagation can take ~2-3m.

Connor O'Brien: after 1% canary pass -> expand
